title: Memory System
description: The Memory interface persists deployment state to disk, a database, or any backend.
overview_body: Every <0>render()</0> call requires a <1>Memory</1> implementation. The runtime calls <2>getState</2> at startup to restore previous state and <3>saveState</3> after each render cycle to persist changes. CReact does not provide a built-in implementation. You bring your own.
method_get_state_desc: Retrieve previously saved state. Return <0>null</0> on first run.
method_save_state_desc: Persist the current state after each render cycle.
method_acquire_lock_desc: Acquire a deploy lock to prevent concurrent deploys. Returns <0>true</0> if acquired.
method_append_audit_log_desc: Append an entry to the deployment audit trail.
method_get_audit_log_desc: Retrieve audit log entries for a stack.
deployment_state_body: The state object contains the full snapshot of all nodes, their props, outputs, and deployment status.
lifecycle_step_render: <0>render()</0> is called with your Memory implementation
lifecycle_step_get_state: Runtime calls <0>memory.getState(stackName)</0> to load previous state
lifecycle_step_hydrate_outputs: Saved output values are hydrated into nodes (so child components can read them immediately)
lifecycle_step_diff: The reconciler diffs new JSX tree against saved nodes
lifecycle_step_rerun: Handlers re-run for all nodes to re-establish side effects. They receive previously saved outputs and should be idempotent
lifecycle_step_save_state: After the cycle, <0>memory.saveState(stackName, state)</0> persists the result
crash_recovery_body: If the process crashes mid-deploy, <0>applyingNodeId</0> records which node was in progress. On restart, the runtime resumes from that node instead of starting over.
enables_crash_recovery: <0>Crash recovery:</0> nodes with saved outputs are treated as deployed
enables_incremental_deploys: <0>Incremental deploys:</0> only changed resources are re-applied
enables_drift_detection: <0>Drift detection:</0> compare expected state vs actual
enables_concurrency_protection: <0>Concurrency protection:</0> optional locking prevents parallel deploys
enables_audit_trail: <0>Audit trail:</0> optional logging tracks deployment history
backend_options_intro: The interface works with any storage backend. Common implementations:
backend_dynamodb_desc: Serverless, low-latency, supports conditional writes for locking
backend_redis_desc: Fast, TTL-based cleanup for short-lived stacks
tip_minimal_example: For a minimal working example, see <0>Getting Started: State and Memory</0>.
